items.front: What restriction applies when giving route or altitude changes to aircraft carrying dangerous materials?
items.back: Advise the pilot of the proposed change and why it is needed; if giving an en route descent, do not vector the aircraft off its planned route unless the pilot concurs.

items.front: When may celestial navigation training be approved in the conterminous United States?
items.back: Only for USAF or USN aircraft that are transponder-equipped and operating within ARTCC radar coverage.

items.front: Once special handling is approved for an experimental aircraft, when may ATC ask it to deviate from planned action?
items.back: Only to preclude an emergency situation.

items.front: When may special procedures be approved for FAA research and development test flights?
items.back: Only when they are coordinated in advance, traffic permits, and the facility air traffic manager has approved the special procedures.

items.front: What must the first facility contacting a FLYNET aircraft do?
items.back: Provide expeditious handling, report it to the OS or CIC for DEN reporting, and note in the flight plan remarks that DEN notification has been completed.

items.front: When does MARSA separation responsibility shift on designated IFR military training routes?
items.back: For designated MARSA routes, the military assumes separation responsibility after the aircraft passes the entry fix until ATC reestablishes separation after the MTR operation is complete.

items.front: How should interceptor aircraft on active air defense scrambles be handled?
items.back: Provide maximum assistance and, when requested, expedite transfer of control jurisdiction to the requesting ADCF.

items.front: What must ATC do with reports of unusual aircraft activity near special interest sites?
items.back: Immediately relay the information to supervisory or CIC personnel.

items.front: What is the key rule about Special Flight Rules Areas?
items.back: Special flight rules are prescribed in 14 CFR part 93, and all SFRAs are subject to those special rules unless ATC authorizes otherwise.

items.front: What is required for operations in the DC SFRA security services area?
items.back: Two-way radio communications, flight planning, and an operational transponder on an assigned code are required.

items.front: What must a controller do upon receiving a SECNOT?
items.back: Forward all information on the aircraft to the OS or CIC. If information is not known, broadcast the call sign on all frequencies and advise the OS or CIC of any response.

items.front: How are law enforcement aircraft handled when they request assistance?
items.back: Provide maximum assistance and, when requested by the crew, use a separate and unique communications frequency whenever possible.

items.front: What block-altitude handling applies when authorizing military aerial refueling?
items.back: Authorize refueling along the published or special track and assign the requested refueling block, either as a maintained block or as a block beginning at one altitude and descending to another.

items.front: What is the preferred way to separate two supersonic aircraft above FL600?
items.back: Normally descend the aircraft at the lower flight level and provide vertical separation.

items.front: When are military special-use frequencies assigned?
items.back: Assign them to the listed qualifying military operations, including all aircraft during supersonic flight.

items.front: What must ATC do if a proposed flight path will cross a reported or forecast hazardous-radiation area?
items.back: Advise the pilot and reroute the aircraft if the pilot requests it.

items.front: How are SAMP flights handled?
items.back: Provide special handling and approve inflight route and altitude changes to the maximum extent possible, even if other IFR aircraft must be recleared.

items.front: What is the ATC priority for AWACS or NORAD Special flights?
items.back: Do not delay them; expediting actions may include minor route changes and altitude changes within plus or minus 2,000 feet that do not impede progress to the orbit.

items.front: What traffic information is given to a TEAL or NOAA mission aircraft for dropsonde releases when workload permits?
items.back: Advise of traffic expected to pass below the mission aircraft near the drop, including the traffic's altitude, direction of flight, and ETA at the closest point or drop fix.

items.front: When may an evasive action maneuver be approved?
items.back: Only when traffic conditions permit it, and ATC specifies the route segment, maximum centerline deviation, and altitude limits as needed.

items.front: What must controllers know before handling nonstandard formation or cell operations?
items.back: They should know the tactics used in the nonstandard cell operation and the procedures that support it.
